capabilities.name: github
capabilities.title: GitHub
capabilities.oneLiner: Read repos, issues and PRs — every tool becomes a capability.
capabilities.author: Krelvan

capabilities.name: filesystem
capabilities.title: Filesystem
capabilities.oneLiner: Give an agent scoped read/write access to a local folder.
capabilities.author: Krelvan

capabilities.name: hn.top
capabilities.title: Hacker News
capabilities.oneLiner: Fetch the current top stories from Hacker News.
capabilities.author: Krelvan
capabilities.yaml:
name: hn.top
description: Fetch the current top story ids from Hacker News
sideEffect: read
estimateCents: 0
http:
  url: https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0/topstories.json
  method: GET

capabilities.name: weather.fetch
capabilities.title: Weather
capabilities.oneLiner: Current forecast for any latitude/longitude — no key needed.
capabilities.author: Krelvan
capabilities.yaml:
name: weather.fetch
description: Fetch the current weather for a location (Open-Meteo, no key)
sideEffect: read
estimateCents: 0
http:
  url: https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude={{input.lat}}&longitude={{input.lon}}&current_weather=true
  method: GET
input:
  lat:
    type: string
    required: true
  lon:
    type: string
    required: true
responseField: current_weather

capabilities.name: wikipedia.summary
capabilities.title: Wikipedia
capabilities.oneLiner: Get the summary extract for any Wikipedia article.
capabilities.author: Krelvan
capabilities.yaml:
name: wikipedia.summary
description: Fetch the summary of a Wikipedia article
sideEffect: read
estimateCents: 0
http:
  url: https://en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/page/summary/{{input.title}}
  method: GET
input:
  title:
    type: string
    required: true

capabilities.name: discord.notify
capabilities.title: Discord notify
capabilities.oneLiner: Post a message to a Discord channel via webhook.
capabilities.author: community
capabilities.yaml:
name: discord.notify
description: Post a message to a Discord channel webhook
sideEffect: message-human
estimateCents: 0
http:
  url: "{{secret:DISCORD_WEBHOOK}}"
  method: POST
  body:
    content: "{{input.message}}"
input:
  message:
    type: string
    required: true

capabilities.name: serp.search
capabilities.title: SERP Search (Pro)
capabilities.oneLiner: Google-grade web search results via a managed SERP API.
capabilities.author: marketplace
capabilities.yaml:
name: serp.search
description: Web search results via SerpApi (requires a SERP_API_KEY)
sideEffect: read
estimateCents: 0
http:
  url: https://serpapi.com/search.json?q={{input.q}}&api_key={{secret:SERP_API_KEY}}
  method: GET
input:
  q:
    type: string
    required: true

capabilities.name: price-monitor
capabilities.title: Price Monitor
capabilities.oneLiner: Watch a page on a schedule, detect price changes vs last run, and alert you — with a signed record of every check.
capabilities.author: Krelvan
capabilities.recommendedModel: a capable model (e.g. claude-sonnet, gpt-4o, or qwen2.5:14b on Ollama)

manifest.name: Price Monitor
manifest.intent: Watch a product/price page on a schedule, detect when the price changes versus the last run, and alert me — keeping a signed record of every check.

nodes.role: Load the last recorded price for this page from memory (recall.last_price). On the very first run this will be empty.
Fetch the current contents of {{input.target_url}} so the price can be read.

You are a price-watch analyst. Read TWO things: (A) the CURRENT DATA TO ANALYZE section contains the freshly-fetched page text — extract the price the page shows RIGHT NOW from there. (B) the MEMORY section contains 'last_price (from a PREVIOUS run)' — that is the OLD price from before. If the MEMORY section is absent or has no last_price, this is the FIRST run. Steps: 1) From the CURRENT page text only, extract the current price as a bare number string, e.g. "39.99" (no symbols/words). Put it in current_price. 2) Take the previous price from MEMORY's last_price. 3) Set changed to true ONLY if a previous last_price existed AND it is different from the current price; otherwise false. Rules: write prices as quoted strings; NEVER copy the memory value into current_price — current_price must come from the CURRENT page text; never invent a previous price like 0.00. Output object keys: current_price (the price on the page now, as a quoted string), changed (true/false), result (one sentence, e.g. 'Price dropped from 49.99 to 39.99' / 'No change, still 39.99' / 'First run, baseline set to 49.99').
Post the price-change summary to the operator.
Persist the current price as the new baseline for the next run.

capabilities.name: slack.post
capabilities.yaml:
name: slack.post
description: Post a message to a Slack channel using a Bot User OAuth token (chat.postMessage). More capable than an incoming webhook — one bot token posts to any channel it is invited to.
sideEffect: message-human
estimateCents: 1

http:
  url: "https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage"
  method: POST
  headers:
    Authorization: "Bearer {{secret:slack-bot-token}}"
    Content-Type: "application/json; charset=utf-8"
  body:
    channel: "{{input.channel}}"
    text: "{{input.message}}"

input:
  channel:
    type: string
    required: true
    description: Channel ID (e.g. C0123456789) or name the bot has been invited to.
  message:
    type: string
    required: true
    description: The message text to post (Slack mrkdwn supported).

# Slack returns HTTP 200 even on logical errors; the {ok:false} body is surfaced as the response.
responseField: ok

successCodes:
  - 200

capabilities.name: firecrawl.scrape
capabilities.yaml:
name: firecrawl.scrape
description: Scrape a single public URL to clean markdown using Firecrawl. Strips nav/ads/boilerplate so an agent can reason over real content. Use for price pages, articles, product pages.
sideEffect: read
estimateCents: 2

http:
  url: "https://api.firecrawl.dev/v2/scrape"
  method: POST
  headers:
    Authorization: "Bearer {{secret:firecrawl-api-key}}"
    Content-Type: "application/json"
  body:
    url: "{{input.url}}"
    formats:
      - markdown

input:
  url:
    type: string
    required: true
    description: The public URL to scrape (must be publicly reachable).

# Firecrawl returns { success, data: { markdown, metadata, ... } }.
responseField: data.markdown

successCodes:
  - 200

capabilities.name: kb-ingest
capabilities.title: Knowledge Base Ingest
capabilities.oneLiner: Load a document into a named knowledge base so a RAG agent can answer from it.
capabilities.author: Krelvan

manifest.name: Knowledge Base Ingest
manifest.intent: Add a document to a named knowledge base so a RAG agent can answer questions grounded in it.

nodes.role: Chunk and embed the provided text into the knowledge base.

capabilities.name: support-bot
capabilities.title: RAG Support Bot
capabilities.oneLiner: Answer questions grounded ONLY in your ingested docs, cite the source, refuse when it doesn't know — with a signed record.
capabilities.author: Krelvan
capabilities.recommendedModel: a capable model (claude-sonnet, gpt-4o, or qwen2.5:14b on Ollama) + an embeddings model (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small or Ollama nomic-embed-text)

manifest.name: RAG Support Bot
manifest.intent: Answer a question from my ingested knowledge base, grounded only in the retrieved documents, and cite the source — with a signed record of every answer.

seed.query: How do I get a refund?

nodes.role: Retrieve the most relevant knowledge-base chunks for the question.

You are a support agent. The retrieved knowledge-base context is in the CURRENT DATA TO ANALYZE section (key ending in .body) — each chunk is tagged with its source like [1] (source: handbook). The user's question is in the 'query' state value. Answer the question using ONLY the retrieved context. If the context does not contain the answer, say you don't have that information — do NOT make anything up. Output object keys: result (your answer to the user, one or two sentences, grounded in the context); grounded (true if the answer came from the context, false if the context lacked the answer); cited_source (the source tag of the chunk you used, e.g. 'handbook', or 'none').
Record this Q&A to memory for an audit trail.
